query_theme: space earth geology ocean depth scale maps infrastructure systems science engineering curiosity
topic_doctrine: Visualized is a faceless EVERGREEN visual-curiosity channel. One broad question, one tension, one memorable reveal, one visual experience per video — a museum exhibit in motion, never a lecture and never news. Topics must score on the six pillars (impossible scale, hidden systems, what-if scenarios, extreme comparisons, process journeys, maps & layers), stay relevant for years, and become MORE compelling when seen. Every number is real and traceable to a source; the notes field of each dataset says exactly where it came from. If the narration were muted, the visual story must still feel worth watching. Full doctrine: data_learning/CURIOSITY_BRAIN.md.

stories.title: The Deepest Hole on Earth Is Welded Shut
stories.hook: We dug for 20 years. Then Earth said no.
stories.closing: 12 kilometers down — and we never left the first layer.
stories.question: How deep would you dare to go? Drop your limit below.
stories.caption: Humans spent 20 years drilling the deepest hole on Earth — 12,262 meters, deeper than the ocean's deepest trench — and still only made it a third of the way through the crust. At 180°C the rock itself fought back, and in 1992 the planet won. The hole is welded shut today. How deep would you dare to go? 👇

segments.say: The deepest any human has ever traveled: 10,935 meters, the bottom of the ocean. But a hole in Russia goes deeper — the Kola Superdeep Borehole, 12,262 meters straight down.
Here's the catch — Earth's crust runs about 35,000 meters deep. Twenty years of drilling got us to 12,262. Barely a third of the way through the planet's THINNEST layer.
So why stop? Heat. Scientists expected 100 degrees Celsius down there — the rock was 180. Drill bits went soft, and in 1992 the planet won. The hole is welded shut today.

stories.title: You're Moving 828,000 km/h Right Now
stories.hook: You're moving faster than a bullet right now!
stories.closing: The couch is the fastest vehicle you own.
stories.question: Did you feel any of that? Tell me below.
stories.caption: You think you're sitting still — but Earth's spin is carrying you at 1,670 km/h, its orbit at 107,000 km/h, and the galaxy is dragging you at 828,000 km/h. That's faster than the fastest machine humans ever built. Did you feel any of it? 👇

segments.say: Right now Earth's spin is carrying you at 1,670 km/h — faster than the speed of sound. And that is the SLOWEST ride you're on.
Because the whole planet is orbiting the Sun at 107,000 km/h — about 30 times faster than a rifle bullet's 3,400. And you have felt none of it.
And the Sun drags us around the galaxy at 828,000 km/h — faster than the Parker Solar Probe at 692,000, the fastest machine humans ever built. Sitting still is the fastest thing you do.
